title: Now THIS is how you do a second edition! (Rogue Dungeon 2nd edition - overview and comparison)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CUpAWj1wVbU

Daniel (@dungeondive) is openly delighted by Rogue Dungeon 2nd Edition from Cerys Gate Games — a game he once said he'd publish first if the Dungeon Dive were a publisher, and whose second edition the makers credit to the channel's community. He calls it one of the finest dungeon crawls ever made: a 1–3 player co-op roguelike of hand management, card draw and dice, all in a box barely bigger than two VHS tapes that plays on a TV tray.

This is largely a glowing overview-and-comparison. His one big original gripe — the art and graphic design — is resolved: the new edition is an "HD remaster," the boards thicker, the rules still excellent. He tours the additions: three new heroes (ten total), 24 double-sided dungeons (up from 14), more monsters per tier, a whole new Tomb deck with cursed gear, and a surprise mega-dungeon mode. The persistent-room system (yellow rooms you re-face each pass) still shines, and the mix of combat, traps, NPCs and events is everything he wants from a small-box crawl. A model second edition; he hopes for small expansions next.

----

Rogue Dungeon's second edition exists largely because a community rallied around a scrappy Game Crafter original. What's a small or self-published game you'd love to see get the full "HD remaster" treatment?
